page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 16 dedicated vCPUs, mapped from 8 physical Intel processor cores running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention, making it a stable option for workloads requiring predictable execution times.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8ib.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for standard enterprise applications. It is designed for workloads such as web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and medium-sized databases that require a steady balance of compute power and memory without the need for specialized hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical Intel cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration establishes a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage options. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance for standard enterprise workloads. This instance is suited for general-purpose applications, including web serving, containerized microservices, and application servers.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual instance featuring dedicated Intel processors and high-speed network connectivity for predictable enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for reliable data transfer
AWS Nitro hypervisor integration for secure and efficient virtualization
